_note: Light Ch 6 articles inline. Arts 625 (EV), 680 (pools), 690 (PV), 695 (fire pumps) have dedicated per-article files.

articles.scope: Electric signs (fixed and portable), outline lighting (including neon and skeleton tubing). Branch circuit dedicated (600.5(A)); GFCI for portable signs (600.10(C)); secondary wiring (600.32 — neon).

key_sections.title: Branch Circuits
key_sections.summary: Each commercial occupancy accessible to pedestrians and at least 20 A dedicated sign circuit

key_sections.title: Grounding and Bonding
key_sections.summary: Sign and metal equipment must be grounded; bonding for neon/skeleton tubing through GTO cable

common_errors: Forgetting the 20 A dedicated sign branch circuit at each commercial occupancy entry
articles.divergence_notes: Art 600 is NEC-specific. IEC has no equivalent dedicated article — sign wiring handled via standard wiring methods + IEC 60598 for luminaires.
articles.usage_notes: Default to a dedicated 20 A circuit per occupancy front for sign use.

articles.article_title: Elevators, Dumbwaiters, Escalators, Moving Walks, Platform Lifts, and Stairway Chairlifts
articles.scope: Elevator/escalator branch circuits, disconnect (620.51), feeder, GFCI for machine room receptacles (620.85), MoR (machine room) circuits.

key_sections.title: Branch Circuits for Other than Elevator Power
key_sections.summary: Car lighting, car receptacles, machine room lighting and receptacles all on separate branch circuits (not on the elevator power circuit)

key_sections.title: Disconnecting Means
key_sections.summary: Disconnect for each elevator within sight of the controller; lockable; identified

key_sections.title: Ground-Fault Circuit-Interrupter Protection for Personnel
key_sections.summary: GFCI required for 125 V, 15- and 20-A receptacles in elevator pits, machine rooms, escalator wellways

common_errors: Putting car lighting on the elevator power feeder — must be a separate branch circuit per 620.22
related_iec_60364: IEC 60364-7-708 (Caravan parks, camping sites and similar locations) — peripheral
EN 81 series (Elevators) — primary reference outside NEC
articles.divergence_notes: NEC Art 620 is comprehensive for elevators; IEC defers to EN 81 series (lift equipment) for most elevator-specific rules.
articles.usage_notes: Coordinate with the elevator vendor's submittal — vendor specifies the controller's exact feeder and branch-circuit needs.

articles.scope: IT rooms (data centres, server rooms, telecomm equipment rooms) meeting 645.4 conditions can use the optional rules of Art 645 — special wiring methods (under-floor wiring, abandoned cable), special disconnect requirements, optional zone-protection.

key_sections.title: Special Requirements for Information Technology Equipment Room
key_sections.summary: All five conditions must be met to use Art 645 (disconnecting means at exits, fire suppression, room construction, etc.)

key_sections.title: Disconnecting Means
key_sections.summary: Disconnect at each principal exit to shut down IT equipment power and HVAC simultaneously (the 'EPO' button)

common_errors: Trying to use Art 645 optional rules without meeting all five 645.4 conditions — invalidates the entire article's permissions
articles.divergence_notes: Art 645's 'opt-in' approach is unique to NEC — meet the conditions and gain permission to use optional wiring methods. IEC handles data-centre power through TIA-942 and dedicated IEC 60364-7-740 (partial scope).
articles.usage_notes: Document the five 645.4 conditions explicitly on the design narrative if claiming Art 645 permissions.

articles.scope: Industrial machines per NFPA 79 (Electrical Standard for Industrial Machinery). Disconnect, nameplate, SCCR, supply conductors.

key_sections.title: Nameplate Data
key_sections.summary: Industrial machine nameplate must include: supply voltage, FLA, SCCR, max OCPD ahead of machine, etc.

key_sections.title: Short-Circuit Current Rating
key_sections.summary: Machine SCCR must equal or exceed available fault current at point of supply

common_errors: Specifying a machine with SCCR < available fault current — common failure on retrofits
related_iec_60364: IEC 60204-1 (Safety of machinery — Electrical equipment of machines) — primary IEC reference for industrial machinery
articles.divergence_notes: Art 670 + NFPA 79 ≈ IEC 60204-1; conceptually aligned but specific values and certifications differ.
articles.usage_notes: Cross-reference NFPA 79 for machine-internal wiring; NEC 670 covers the supply side.

articles.scope: EV charging — Levels 1/2/3, 125% continuous sizing, GFCI, ventilation, load management.

articles.article_title: Swimming Pools, Fountains, and Similar Installations
articles.scope: Pools, spas, hot tubs, fountains — GFCI rules, equipotential bonding, receptacle clearances.
PV system definitions, circuit sizing, max voltage, DC arc fault, rapid shutdown, grounding.
Fire pump power source, supply continuity, controller, conductor sizing, disconnect prohibition.
